title: AskUserQuestion guard auto-fixes an over-long header and lets the call proceed, reporting what changed
action:
Per ask-guard-autofix D1/D2. checkAskUserQuestion (skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/guards.mjs:752) currently DENIES a header >12 chars. Change: a >12-char header is a FIXABLE violation — rewrite it to the first 11 chars, right-trimmed, plus '…' (total ≤12) on a deep-cloned copy of toolInput; collect a human-readable note per fix naming old → new. Unfixable violations (question count, option count, missing label/description) keep their exact current deny — and DENY WINS: if the same call has both a fixable and an unfixable violation, return the deny. When only fixes occurred return a new verdict shape { allow: true, fixed: <whole replacement toolInput>, notes: [...] }; untouched valid input keeps returning { allow: true } with no fixed field.

ADAPTER (hooks/bee-write-guard.mjs, deny emission is at :734-741 and stays byte-identical for denies): when the verdict carries fixed, print to STDOUT one JSON object { hookSpecificOutput: { hookEventName: 'PreToolUse', permissionDecision: 'allow', permissionDecisionReason: <notes joined>, updatedInput: <verdict.fixed>, additionalContext: 'bee AskUserQuestion guard auto-fixed: <notes>' }, systemMessage: 'bee AskUserQuestion guard: <notes>' } and exit 0. updatedInput replaces tool input WHOLLY (D2, confirmed against the current Claude Code hooks doc). Keep the guard's whole-function fail-open try/catch: any throw in the fix path degrades to plain allow.

TESTS (follow the existing rows in test_guards.mjs:120-138): the long-header row now asserts the fixed verdict — exact truncated value 'Worktree sw…' for input 'Worktree switch'; add rows for multi-question input where one header is long (only that header rewritten, others byte-identical), fixable+unfixable together => deny, exactly-12-char header untouched, and fail-open rows unchanged. Extend the adapter-level test (hooks/test_write_guard.mjs and/or skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_write_guard_hook.mjs, whichever already exercises AskUserQuestion denies) with one row proving the stdout JSON emission: exit 0, parseable JSON, permissionDecision allow, updatedInput.questions[0].header === 'Worktree sw…'.

MIRRORS: byte-mirror lib change to .bee/bin/lib/guards.mjs and hook change to .bee/bin/hooks/bee-write-guard.mjs; re-render whatever the manifest covers so release_manifest --check and ledger_parity --check end green (the repo's own regen flow, see the manifest script — never hand-edit rendered trees).
verify: node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_guards.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_write_guard_hook.mjs && node hooks/test_write_guard.mjs && node scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs && node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --check && node scripts/ledger_parity.mjs --check
must_haves: input 'Worktree switch' (15 chars) is rewritten to 'Worktree sw…' and the call is ALLOWED with the rewritten input
a call that mixes a fixable and an unfixable violation still denies with the current message
the emitted stdout JSON carries permissionDecision 'allow' and the WHOLE replacement toolInput under updatedInput
deny path (exit 2 + stderr) byte-unchanged for unfixable violations
release_manifest --check and ledger_parity --check green at cap

trace.outcome: AskUserQuestion guard header>12 auto-fixes to first-11-chars-trimmed+'…', proceeds allowed with updatedInput+notes; unfixable violations still deny (deny wins); mirrors + manifest/ledger regen'd

{
  "tests_added": [
    "skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_guards.mjs: checkAskUserQuestion over-long-header row rewritten to assert allow+fixed+notes ('Worktree switch' -> 'Worktree sw…'), original input never mutated",
    "skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_guards.mjs: new row — multi-question call rewrites only the long header, other question byte-identical",
    "skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_guards.mjs: new row — fixable header + unfixable option-count violation still denies with the unfixable reason (deny wins)",
    "skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_guards.mjs: new row — exactly-12-char header left untouched, no fixed field",
    "hooks/test_write_guard.mjs: adapter-level row — AskUserQuestion with a 16-char header exits 0, emits parseable stdout JSON, permissionDecision 'allow', updatedInput.questions[0].header === 'Worktree sw…'",
    "hooks/test_write_guard.mjs: adapter-level row — fixable header alongside an unfixable violation still denies (exit 2) naming the unfixable reason"
  ],
  "red_failure_evidence": "Before the fix, checkAskUserQuestion(...) for header 'Worktree switch' (15 chars) returned {allow:false, kind:'ask-schema', reason:'bee AskUserQuestion guard: header \"Worktree switch\" is 15 chars — max 12 ... Shorten the header.'} — a hard deny, not an auto-fix. Running the new/changed test_guards.mjs rows against the pre-fix code produced: 'FAIL checkAskUserQuestion turns opaque \"Invalid tool parameters\" into a clear, specific deny; fail-open on odd shapes / an over-long header must be auto-fixed and allowed, not denied, got {\"allow\":false,...}'; 'FAIL checkAskUserQuestion: multi-question call only rewrites the long header ... got {\"allow\":false,...}'; 'FAIL checkAskUserQuestion: a fixable header alongside an unfixable violation still denies with the unfixable reason (deny wins) / the deny reason must name the unfixable violation, got bee AskUserQuestion guard: header \"Deploy staging\" is 14 chars (question 1) — max 12 ...' (3 failed / 31 passed). At the adapter level, hooks/test_write_guard.mjs's new AskUserQuestion-autofix row against the pre-fix hook produced: 'FAIL - AskUserQuestion with a 16-char header is auto-fixed and allowed (exit 0) :: status=2 stderr=bee AskUserQuestion guard: header \"Worktree switch\" is 15 chars — max 12 ...'; 'FAIL - the auto-fix emits parseable JSON on stdout'; 'FAIL - the auto-fix stdout carries permissionDecision allow'; 'FAIL - the auto-fix stdout carries the whole rewritten toolInput with the truncated header'; 'FAIL - the deny message names the unfixable option-count violation :: ...header \"Worktree switch\" is 15 chars...' (5 failures) — the old code denied both the pure-fixable call AND the mixed fixable+unfixable call for the SAME (wrong) reason: the header check, since it returned early on hit, never let the loop reach the real unfixable violation.",
  "verify_output_tail": "PASS test_lib_mirror: templates/lib and .bee/bin/lib are byte-identical (29 files)\nPASS test_lib_mirror: runtime-derived hook inventory is byte-identical (10 files)\nrelease_manifest --check: 499 file(s) match stored manifest\nledger_parity --check: .bee/bin/** matches the .bee/onboarding.json managed-hash ledger"
}
